formal_definition: The Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT), a short-dated, digitally native UK government gilt HM Treasury is piloting on a platform operating within the Digital Securities Sandbox, kept independent of the government's main debt-management programme. On 12 February 2026 HM Treasury announced HSBC, using its Orion digital-assets platform, as the selected platform provider following an Invitation to Tender published in October 2025, with Ashurst LLP named as legal adviser; no issuance date had been announced as of that update.
plain_definition: HM Treasury's pilot to issue a UK government bond (gilt) natively as a digital token rather than through the normal paper-based/registry process, using the Digital Securities Sandbox as the legal environment. It is a standalone pilot, kept separate from the government's main debt-management programme, not a change to how ordinary gilts are issued.
